title: WebAssembly-Based Sandboxing
concept: Running sandboxed code directly in the browser environment, avoiding backend infrastructure requirements.

exercises.prompt: What do we capture from a code execution sandbox to review the results?

options.text: stdout, stderr, exit codes, and file diffs
Just the exit codes and stdout

exercises.explain: We capture stdout, stderr, exit codes, and file diffs to fully understand what happened during execution.

cards.title: Running Code in Your Browser
cards.body: WebAssembly allows us to run code inside the browser without needing a server. It's like having a mini-computer right on your webpage!

cards.title: What is WebAssembly?
cards.body: WebAssembly (or wasm) is a binary instruction format that lets you run code in the browser efficiently. It helps in creating performance-sensitive applications.

cards.title: Think of a Browser as a Kitchen
cards.body: Imagine the browser as a kitchen where you can cook (run) recipes (code). WebAssembly lets you make complex dishes in the kitchen without needing special equipment (servers)!

cards.title: Example in Action
cards.body: When you use an app like a game in your browser, WebAssembly allows the game to run quickly without constantly checking back with a server.

cards.title: Myth: Sandboxes are Slow
cards.body: Some think running code in a sandbox is always slower. With WebAssembly, sandboxes can run fast and efficiently, almost like native code!

cards.title: WebAssembly Flow
cards.body: See how WebAssembly runs code in the browser?

left.text: Code runs directly in the browser.

exercises.prompt: What does WebAssembly allow you to do?

options.text: Run code directly in the server
Run sandboxed code directly in the browser
Run code only on mobile devices

exercises.explain: WebAssembly lets us run sandboxed code in the browser without needing any heavy server setup.

exercises.prompt: WebAssembly can run code directly in the browser without backend infrastructure. True or False?
exercises.explain: That's true! WebAssembly enables running code in the browser without needing a backend.

exercises.prompt: WebAssembly is a technology that allows running __________ directly in the browser.
exercises.sentence: WebAssembly is a technology that allows running code directly in the browser.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'code', which is what WebAssembly executes in the browser.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following statements about WebAssembly is generated by an AI?

options.text: WebAssembly does not require any backend servers.
WebAssembly is only used in mobile apps.
WebAssembly makes websites slower and less responsive.

exercises.explain: The correct statement is about WebAssembly not requiring backend servers, showcasing its efficiency.

exercises.prompt: What is a main benefit of using WebAssembly for running sandboxed applications in browsers?

options.text: It allows running faster, sandboxed code directly in browsers without needing backend resources.
It limits what developers can build.
It is only suitable for small, simple apps.

exercises.explain: The main benefit is that WebAssembly enables faster, sandboxed code execution directly in browsers, avoiding the need for backend servers.
